name: Bridgerton
description: Netflix's Regency-era drama — the Bridgerton family, the ton, and Lady Whistledown.

questions.q: Bridgerton is based on a series of Regency romance novels written by which author?

source.notes: Quinn's eight novels, published 2000-2006.

questions.q: On which streaming service did Bridgerton originally premiere in December 2020?

source.notes: Produced by Shonda Rhimes's Shondaland.

questions.q: How many children are there in the Bridgerton family?

source.notes: Anthony, Benedict, Colin, Daphne, Eloise, Francesca, Gregory, Hyacinth — alphabetical.

questions.q: Which Bridgerton sibling is the focus of Season 1's romance?

source.notes: Daphne and Simon Basset, the Duke of Hastings.

questions.q: The Duke of Hastings, Daphne's Season 1 love interest, goes by what first name?

source.notes: Played by Regé-Jean Page.

questions.q: In Season 1, Queen Charlotte names Daphne which honorific for being the 'Diamond of the First Water'?

source.notes: 'Diamond' is also the Season 1 opening-episode title.

questions.q: Which Shondaland showrunner created Bridgerton for Netflix?

source.notes: Van Dusen created the show; Rhimes is EP. Jess Brownell took over for Season 3.

questions.q: Who voices the anonymous society gossip Lady Whistledown?

source.notes: Andrews narrates without appearing on screen.

questions.q: Which Bridgerton sibling is the 9th Viscount and head of the family after his father's death?

source.notes: Anthony is the eldest; played by Jonathan Bailey.

questions.q: Which actress plays Queen Charlotte?

source.notes: India Amarteifio plays the younger Charlotte in the spin-off.

questions.q: Season 2 focuses on Anthony Bridgerton and which new character?

source.notes: Kate Sharma (Simone Ashley) supplants the book's Kate Sheffield.

questions.q: Which actress plays Kate Sharma in Season 2?

source.notes: Ashley previously appeared in Sex Education.

questions.q: Season 3 centers on which Bridgerton sibling's romance?

source.notes: Out of book order (Quinn's Book 4).

questions.q: Which young actress plays Penelope Featherington — a role that earned her Emmy buzz?

source.notes: Coughlan also stars in Derry Girls.

questions.q: Who plays eldest sibling Anthony Bridgerton?

source.notes: Bailey is a BAFTA winner for Wicked (2024).

questions.q: Which iconic wise patroness mentors the Bridgerton siblings and the Sharmas throughout the series?

source.notes: Played by Adjoa Andoh.

questions.q: Who plays Violet, the Bridgerton family matriarch?

source.notes: Polly Walker plays Portia Featherington.

questions.q: What prestige status object is repeatedly used to decorate ballrooms — a mark of wealth in Bridgerton's Regency?

source.notes: Fresh pineapples were rare, expensive status symbols in Regency Britain.

questions.q: In May 2023, Bridgerton spawned a prequel on Netflix that was titled what?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: date fixed from December 2023 to May 2023 — focused on the young Queen Charlotte.

questions.q: The string-quartet covers used for ballroom dances often reinterpret what kind of music?

source.notes: E.g. Ariana Grande's 'thank u, next,' Taylor Swift's 'Wildest Dreams.'

questions.q: Which composer scores the show, blending classical strings with modern sensibility?

source.notes: Bowers also scored Green Book and King Richard.

questions.q: Lady Whistledown's real identity was revealed at the end of Season 1. Who is she?

source.notes: Quiet, bookish Penelope Featherington.
